subject: LU-7408 target: declare write for reply data

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/35595/

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

1 style warning(s).
For more details please see https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Coding+Guidelines

Patch Set 1:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/35595/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

PPC64 Builder: build succesful

Patch Set 1:

if transaction didn't happen, then who will release the slot ?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Can't land patch to master without LU- ticket.

Also, is there a reason this patch is dependent on http://review.whamcloud.com/17085 ?  It doesn't seem related.

Patch Set 1:

>>> if transaction didn't happen, then who will release the slot ?

let's filter out the empty slot because the entries are of fixed size, will it work?

Patch Set 1:

sorry, I don't understand. tgt_find_free_reply_slot() reserves a slot, then it's filled for a specific XID and then the client reports when the slot can be released (so reused). if the client doesn't get the slot and the server doesn't release it on its own, then the slot is lost.
I guess this is yet another case where append declaration would help. right now it's better to just declare the record at big enough offset.
which is unfortunate for ldiskfs, but that' s it.

Patch Set 1:

wait, if the trans doesn't happen, it won't fill anything in the slot. That means only bitmap in memory is updated and we can just clear it at the end of trans.

Of course this may generate zero-filled slots on disk but it can be filtered out easily due to its fixed size, and this can only happen in recovery.

Patch Set 1:

> wait, if the trans doesn't happen, it won't fill anything in the
 > slot. That means only bitmap in memory is updated and we can just
 > clear it at the end of trans.

correct. but this is missing in the patch, right? that would require some flag@env which is not very nice. I think declaration at a large offset would be enough as it's just credits we're concerned of.


 > 
 > Of course this may generate zero-filled slots on disk but it can be
 > filtered out easily due to its fixed size, and this can only happen
 > in recovery.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 2:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 2:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/35610/

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/35610/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 2:

PPC64 Builder: build succesful

Patch Set 2:

I find the approach taken very risky.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 2:

> I find the approach taken very risky.

Can you please describe the risks in detail?

Patch Set 2:

the risk is to lose entries in some cases. which will be hard to reproduce and debug. especially after some time and changes to the code. the decision is taken at one place while action at another. this is always a risk, especially with Lustre. finally, what an exact declaration does buy us? we need undefined declarations anyway to make things like LLOG working with ZFS debug.

Patch Set 2:

>  the decision is taken at one place while action at another. this is always a risk, especially with Lustre.

This is all MD model of declare_write() and write() about, isn't it ;-)

I don't know why this issue is resemble to LLOG problem. The LLOG problem is about append write and interference among different writers. But in this problem indices can be reused that means the offset isn't always increased. I tend to think this is a good solution but I may have missed something.

Patch Set 2:

for llog we need undefined declarations anyway. so, it must be a part of the api. so it should be fine for others to use this capability when needed. the fix you provide can work, sure. but there is a simpler and more reliable solution.

Patch Set 2:

Patch Set 2:

>> but there is a simpler and more reliable solution.

What would the proposed solution be? Please describe it in detail, thanks.

Patch Set 2:

Tests uploaded by Shadow Autotest from shadow-10vm6 for review-ldiskfs received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/e749ebb8-880b-11e5-95ea-5254006e85c2). Ran 6 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 2:

Tests uploaded by Shadow Autotest from shadow-14vm6 for review-zfs-part-2 received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/9b64430e-8826-11e5-9053-5254006e85c2). Ran 8 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 2:

Tests uploaded by Shadow Autotest from shadow-18vm5 for review-zfs-part-1 received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/83b2f9ca-882c-11e5-b099-5254006e85c2). Ran 9 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 2:

Tests uploaded by Shadow Autotest from shadow-17vm10 for review-dne-part-1 received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/9db550ae-8839-11e5-84a2-5254006e85c2). Ran 7 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 2:

the solution is to declare a record at a big offset.
we can use number of the clients (plus some margin) to calculate max.offset:

#clients * sizeof(*lrd) * max_mod_rpcs_per_client

Patch Set 2: Verified-1

Tests uploaded by Shadow Autotest from shadow-9vm10 for review-dne-part-2 received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/0a6c153a-8843-11e5-9053-5254006e85c2). Ran 15 tests.  1 tests failed: replay-single.

Patch Set 2:

> #clients * sizeof(*lrd) * max_mod_rpcs_per_client

I don't understand why on-disk reply data is related to these parameters. In case I missed something, can you please describe it in detail?

Patch Set 2:

because this essentially defines max.size of the file storing reply data. each client may have upto 8 reqs in flight.

Patch Set 2:

isn't it of fixed size as roughly around sizeof(reply_data) * LUT_REPLY_SLOTS_PER_CHUNK * LUT_REPLY_SLOTS_MAX_CHUNKS?

Patch Set 2:

you can define it this way, but we can easily limit it by the number of clients to save few credits.

Patch Set 2:

I see, so you want to reserve the space that all client could write up. If it has 20K clients and each client can have up to 8 mod RPCs in flight, that will lead to several megabytes reserved for each transaction - isn;t this something you were against ;-). Besides this is hard to do as # of clients and mod_rpcs could be changed at any time, you will need some cushion to make it right. Sorry if I missed something here.

I'm not convinced, can you come up with another reason against my patch ;-)

Patch Set 2:

no, the size is constant 32 bytes, it's offset which is maximum.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

Andreas, I think it should be enough to declare at bigger offset - this is exactly how llog works. when append declaration is ready we can fix this and llog in a single patch.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

when we have ability to declare "append" (which essentially just a declaration at very high offset + additional checks in the debugging code to allow floating writes), then we can replace this call and many calls in llog. and then we'll be able to run in debug mode.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

(2 comments)

I think both approaches for this patch seem reasonable, but I'd prefer to use this version of the patch.  Firstly, it is currently past testing, which is always good.  Secondly, I agree with Alex that since this is only a credits calculation it is less complex, so this method of handling declarations is OK until dmu_tx_hold_append() is implemented.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

in few words - credits are per tx (usually it means per thread) because declaration itself doesn't promise actual change. the caller may abort transaction before it's started even.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

Please add the comment Andreas requested as a follow-on patch.
Thanks.
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 6765d78583a7b144810e63ce8aa1c05d27cb8189

parents.subject: LU-7144 tests: skip scrub/lfsck test under interoperation

commit.subject: LU-7408 target: declare write for reply data
commit.message:
LU-7408 target: declare write for reply data

declare reply_data at max possible offset - this ensures
enough credits reserved.

Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Change-Id: I08466452e1e95b803f316abae777a8c8f4a8626e
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17086
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
